title: China-to-Canada Air-source Heat Pump Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: Canadian consumer and safety-facing information for heat pumps commonly requires English and French presentation, including federal energy-efficiency information where applicable, safety warnings, installation instructions, operating instructions, warranty information, and packaging or consumer labels. The mandatory obligation depends on the applicable federal regulation and provincial consumer/safety law; bilingual content should be controlled as part of the Canadian technical file and certification review.
target_standards: Consumer Packaging and Labelling Act and Regulations
Provincial consumer protection and electrical safety requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese domestic labels, nameplates, installation manuals, and warranty documents are usually in Simplified Chinese and may include export English. They normally do not include Canadian French terminology, province-specific safety warnings, Canadian certification markings, or NRCan-consistent efficiency statements.
rows.gap: Canada-ready artwork and manuals must be rebuilt in English and French, with the same model identity, electrical ratings, refrigerant data, warnings, certification mark, and energy-efficiency claims used in NRCan and certification records. Direct Chinese-to-English export manuals are usually incomplete for Canada.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where federal energy, packaging, provincial consumer, or electrical safety rules require bilingual information; otherwise expected by certification and retail channels
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory Chinese-language domestic information for applicable China sales; not equivalent to Canadian English/French content

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Gap — prepare Canada-specific English/French labels, manuals, warnings, and claims aligned with NRCan filings and Canadian certification records.

source.publisher: Justice Laws Website / Government of Canada

rows.requirement: Minimum Energy Performance — Air-source Heat Pumps
rows.target_requirement: Canada's Energy Efficiency Regulations, 2016 under the Energy Efficiency Act set mandatory minimum energy performance standards for prescribed energy-using products before import or interprovincial shipment. Air-source central heat pumps and related split-system equipment must meet the applicable Canadian efficiency metrics, test method, verification-marking, and reporting requirements before they are imported into Canada for sale or lease.
target_standards: NRCan energy efficiency reporting and verification requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB 21455-2019 for room air conditioners and multi-split systems, GB/T 25127 series for low-ambient-temperature air-source heat pump units, and China Energy Label data for domestic sales. Chinese COP, IPLV, APF, or low-ambient test records are not automatically accepted as Canadian NRCan compliance evidence.
rows.gap: Canadian efficiency categories, metrics, verification marking, and pre-import reporting differ from Chinese GB and China Energy Label practice. Chinese test reports must be mapped to the Canadian product class and usually re-tested or re-rated to the Canadian test method used for NRCan reporting.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory (Energy Efficiency Regulations, 2016 apply before import or interprovincial shipment for prescribed products)
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for applicable Chinese product categories under GB 21455-2019 and China Energy Label rules

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Major gap — China efficiency data is not a substitute for Canadian NRCan minimum-performance, verification-marking, and reporting compliance.

source.publisher: Justice Laws Website / Government of Canada

rows.requirement: Electrical Safety Certification — Heat Pump Equipment
rows.target_requirement: Canadian market access for electrical heat pump equipment is controlled through provincial and territorial electrical safety laws and codes, generally requiring certification or field evaluation by a certification body accredited by the Standards Council of Canada. CSA C22.2 No. 236 for heating and cooling equipment and CSA C22.2 No. 120 for refrigeration equipment are Canadian standard routes used by accredited certification bodies; the mandatory obligation is the applicable Canadian electrical safety law/code and accepted certification mark, not the CSA standard alone.
target_standards: Provincial and territorial electrical safety legislation and codes
rows.china_equivalent: China CCC certification and GB 4706.32 testing address Chinese domestic electrical safety requirements for heat pumps, air conditioners, and dehumidifiers. CCC marks and Chinese test reports are not accepted as Canadian electrical approval marks unless an SCC-accredited certification body has evaluated and listed the product for Canada.
rows.gap: Canadian certification normally requires construction review, component traceability, Canadian deviations, bilingual markings where required, factory follow-up, and an accepted Canadian certification mark. Chinese CCC files can help the technical review but do not replace SCC-accredited certification or field evaluation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory under applicable provincial/territorial electrical safety law or code; CSA C22.2 No. 236 and CSA C22.2 No. 120 are certification-standard routes used by accredited bodies
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory CCC for applicable Chinese domestic electrical appliance categories

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Major gap — obtain Canadian electrical certification or accepted field evaluation; CCC and GB 4706.32 evidence is not sufficient for Canada.

rows.requirement: Refrigeration Equipment Safety — CSA C22.2 No. 120 Route
rows.target_requirement: Where the heat pump configuration is evaluated as refrigeration equipment or contains refrigeration assemblies within the certification scope, accredited certification bodies may apply CSA C22.2 No. 120 in addition to, or instead of, other Canadian appliance standards. The mandatory obligation remains Canadian electrical approval under the applicable code authority; the standard is a technical certification route.
target_standards: Provincial and territorial electrical safety legislation and codes
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese refrigeration-safety and appliance-safety files may reference GB/T 9237, GB 4706.32, component CCC marks, and factory quality files. These do not determine the Canadian certification scope or the applicable Canadian standard selection.
rows.gap: The Canadian certification body must classify the product and choose the applicable Canadian standard set. Refrigerant controls, compressor protection, abnormal operation, enclosure, wiring, and marking requirements may need design changes even when the Chinese product is CCC-certified.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory electrical approval under the applicable Canadian code authority; CSA C22.2 No. 120 is a technical route when selected by the certification body
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory CCC or GB-based domestic approval only where China scope applies

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Gap — the Canadian certification scope and selected CSA C22.2 route must be confirmed by an SCC-accredited body; Chinese refrigeration files are supporting evidence only.

rows.requirement: EnerGuide / NRCan Product Listing and Consumer Information
rows.target_requirement: For regulated energy-using products, Canada's Energy Efficiency Regulations, 2016 require prescribed energy-efficiency reports and verification information before import or interprovincial shipment. NRCan's EnerGuide and product-rating programs publish or support consumer-facing efficiency information for heating and cooling equipment where applicable. The mandatory obligation is the federal regulation and NRCan filing/verification requirement; EnerGuide presentation must match the accepted Canadian rating data.
target_standards: NRCan energy efficiency report submission requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China Energy Label declarations and GB-based efficiency grades are designed for the Chinese market and use Chinese product categories, label artwork, and rating thresholds. They do not create an NRCan product listing, Canadian verification mark, or EnerGuide-compliant consumer disclosure.
rows.gap: Canadian consumer-facing efficiency information must be generated from Canadian compliant ratings, not copied from China Energy Label files. Model numbers, basic model groupings, verification body data, and reported efficiency values must align across import documents, listings, labels, and marketing claims.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory regulatory filing/verification where the heat pump is a prescribed product; EnerGuide presentation applies where NRCan program rules require or support it
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory China Energy Label only for applicable Chinese domestic product categories

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Gap — Canadian NRCan filing and EnerGuide-compatible information must be built from Canadian accepted ratings; Chinese label files are not transferable.

rows.requirement: HFC Refrigerant Controls — Import and Sale
rows.target_requirement: Canada's Ozone-depleting Substances and Halocarbon Alternatives Regulations under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 control HFCs and certain products containing or designed to contain HFCs. Importers must check whether the heat pump, pre-charged refrigerant, bulk refrigerant, servicing refrigerant, and any GWP-based restriction, permit, allowance, or reporting rule applies before import or sale.
target_standards: Ozone-depleting Substances and Halocarbon Alternatives Regulations (SOR/2016-137)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese product files typically identify refrigerant type and charge quantity under GB/T 7725, GB/T 9237, GB/T 25127, or export bill-of-material controls. China documentation may not include Canadian HFC allowance, permit, product prohibition, or importer reporting analysis.
rows.gap: R410A, R32, R454B, R290, and other refrigerant options must be checked against Canadian HFC and halocarbon rules, including whether the import is equipment, a charged product, or a controlled substance. Chinese refrigerant labels and SDS files do not establish Canadian allowance or product-rule compliance.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory (federal HFC and halocarbon controls apply where the substance or product is within scope)
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent; Chinese refrigerant identification and safety standards do not create Canadian HFC allowances or permits

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Major gap for HFC-charged units — importer must confirm Canadian HFC product restrictions, allowances, permits, and reporting before shipment.

source.publisher: Justice Laws Website / Government of Canada
